rationale_for_baseline_tier: A pointing abscess with surrounding cellulitis and mild systemic features needs urgent same-day in-person drainage and antibiotics — the procedure cannot be done remotely — but there is no current sepsis or airway compromise, so T3 (urgent same-day/urgent care) with escalation for sepsis or deep-neck involvement.

escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day in-person assessment for incision & drainage
escalation_edges.rationale: Definitive treatment (drainage) needs a clinician today.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This lump needs to be drained by a clinician today — please attend an urgent care clinic or GP that can do a minor procedure.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading redness, fever >39°C, confusion, dizziness, or unable to keep fluids down
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals systemic sepsis needing IV antibiotics/admission.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads, your fever climbs above 39°C, you feel confused or dizzy, or can't keep fluids down — go to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Difficulty swallowing, drooling, voice change or difficulty breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests deep neck-space infection threatening the airway.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop trouble swallowing, drooling, a change in your voice, or any difficulty breathing — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure the patient can attend for drainage and has a low threshold to seek help if deteriorating overnight.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This boil needs draining by a clinician today, plus antibiotics for the surrounding redness. Don't squeeze it yourself. Watch for spreading redness, high fever, or any swallowing/voice/breathing change.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day GP/urgent care for drainage; ED/000 for sepsis or airway symptoms
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use an after-hours GP/urgent care that can drain it; ED for systemic or airway symptoms.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a pointing cervical furuncle/abscess with cellulitis and systemic features; drainage performed/needed and antibiotics commenced.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home care/antibiotics only with no drainage and no sepsis/airway screen.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The abscess progresses to sepsis, or a missed deep neck-space infection compromises the airway.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that delays drainage of a mature abscess with systemic features.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance for an uncomplicated drainable abscess without sepsis or airway involvement.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate to confirm fluctuance/depth or drain the abscess remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange urgent in-person assessment for drainage plus antibiotics, and safety-net for sepsis and airway involvement.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Managing remotely with antibiotics alone, or advising self-drainage.
